stimulus: According to Hiroshi Tanaka of Kyoto University, the reason maple samaras seem to hover briefly when ______blank is that their winged shape generates a stable leading-edge vortex, prolonging their descent and giving the wind extra time to carry the seeds away from the parent tree.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The word “spinning” occurs in the middle of a clause and isn’t part of a supplement, so no punctuation is needed after it. This becomes clear when the rest of the sentence is simplified: “The reason maple samaras seem to hover briefly when spinning is that their winged shape generates a stable leading-edge vortex.”

Choice A is incorrect because no punctuation is needed after a participle in the middle of a clause. Choice B is incorrect because a semicolon can’t separate a subordinate “when” clause from its main verb. Choice C is incorrect because a colon can’t separate the subject and its predicate in this way.
